authoritative_store.name: linear_intake_receipts
authoritative_store.access: service_role only; RLS enabled; no delete grant

operator_mirror.name: linear_intake_receipts
operator_mirror.rule: Operator convenience and payload-bearing dead-letter only. Missing or stale mirror rows never override Supabase.

columns.name: child_issue_ids
columns.encoding: JSON array of confirmed Linear UUID strings

identity.payload_hash: lowercase SHA-256 of the exact UTF-8 payload_json bytes; the database recomputes and checks this value with pgcrypto

canonical_payload.serialization: Recursive stable JSON with object keys sorted by code point and no insignificant whitespace
canonical_payload.video_number_rule: Positive consecutive integers starting at 1 in canonical array order
canonical_payload.team_scope_rule: The same create payload has the same payload_hash for video, graphics, and both-mode submission; receipt_key supplies the team scope

identity.stable_child_key: the canonical payload child kind plus its zero-based canonical ordinal
identity.retry_rule: Every retry reuses the same expected Linear UUIDs. A duplicate-ID response is followed by a read of that exact UUID and is success only when the issue matches this receipt.
identity.receipt_abandonment_rule: Once a server receipt exists, the browser cannot replace it with a new payload hash. Empty filming plans are rejected before receipt insertion; server dependency fixes retry the immutable receipt.

states.pending: The payload-bearing receipt exists. A fresh pending row is an in-progress workflow and must not be replayed concurrently.
states.partial: At least one expected issue exists but the full expected set is not confirmed. Automatic parent replay is forbidden.
states.failed: The bounded attempt or preflight failed without a confirmed issue set. The full payload remains in this dead-letter row; a normal retry reuses its deterministic IDs and exact payload.
states.created: The parent and exactly payload_json.videos.length unique deterministic child UUIDs have been read back and confirmed for this receipt. This is the only success state.

database_guards: payload_hash is the database-recomputed SHA-256 of the exact canonical payload_json bytes
nonnull parent and every confirmed child ID must be a Linear UUIDv4; child IDs are unique and cannot be removed or replaced
created requires exactly the expected unique child UUID count derived from payload_json.videos
failed or partial to pending increments attempts by exactly one and atomically installs a new structured replay_note
a pending receipt cannot accept another replay claim

replay.automatic_failed_retry: A failed receipt may be claimed by the normal Submit retry. Exact deterministic-ID reads still precede every create, so an ambiguous earlier response cannot create a second issue.
replay.transport: Repost the unchanged payload_json to the same video-form or graphic-form endpoint with the same payload_hash and receipt_key. A partial receipt must first be operator-claimed, then the repost adds only operator_replay_id equal to that claim's private replay_id; this transport field is excluded from canonical payload hashing.
replay.operator_claim_capability: Supabase RLS keeps replay_id private. A pending operator claim resumes only when operator_replay_id exactly matches the UUIDv4 replay_id and the stored receipt, hash, attempts, source state, and confirmed child IDs all validate; missing or wrong capability returns non-200 and cannot reach Linear.
replay.created_duplicate_rule: A created receipt returns the stored confirmed result and performs no Linear mutation.
replay.claim_rule: Claim replay with one compare-and-set update matching receipt_key, source status, and prior attempts; set status=pending, attempts=prior_attempts+1, and a new replay_note in that same update. Zero updated rows means another worker won and this worker must stop.
replay.replay_note_rule: Store a new stable JSON object for every replay. It is bound to receipt_key, payload_hash, source status, and prior attempts; it records requester, timestamp, reason, and exact deterministic-ID read-before-create state. Never append to or reuse the previous note.

replay_note_schema.replay_id: A new UUIDv4 for this claim; it must differ from the prior replay_id
replay_note_schema.requested_at: UTC ISO-8601 timestamp ending in Z

exact_id_readback.confirmed_child_ids: The exact ordered unique UUID array already stored in child_issue_ids

retention.rule: Never prune pending, failed, or partial rows. A created row may be removed only when updated_at is at least 30 days old and a final exact-ID readback succeeds.
